channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: TikTok travel influencers are illegally hitching a ride on Mauritania’s Iron Ore Train
item.description: Mauritania isn’t typically a major tourist destination. But its only railway has recently become the subject of a viral TikTok travel trend: riding the “Iron Ore Train.” This 437-mile journey through the Sahara desert offers dramatic selfie backdrops—and no shortage of controversy.
item.dc:creator: Regina Borsellino, Fast Company

item.title: America’s loss is China’s gain: Beijing is recruiting fired U.S. scientists
item.description: China appears to be pursuing a calculated effort to recruit recently laid-off U.S. scientists—particularly those with expertise in artificial intelligence—to relocate or contribute remotely to research operations based in Shenzhen.
item.dc:creator: Tim Bajarin, Fast Company

item.title: When transformation needs a home
item.description: For some, the concept of a transformation function evokes little more than a program management office. We don’t subscribe to that view.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Intel sells majority stake in Altera to Silver Lake investors in a deal valued at $8.75 billion
item.description: Intel announced Monday that it was selling the majority of its Altera chip business to technology investor Silver Lake in a deal valued at $8.75 billion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Nvidia plans to manufacture AI chips in the US for the first time
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia announced Monday that it will produce its artificial intelligence super computers in the United States for the first time.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Intel to sell 51% Altera stake to Silver Lake amid cost cuts
item.description: Intel Corp. has agreed to sell a 51% stake in its programmable chips unit Altera to Silver Lake Management, making good on plans to start spinning off non-core assets.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: How people secured their secrets before encyption
item.description: Centuries before encrypted texts and secure video conferencing, people relied on physical engineering to keep their written messages sturdy, sealed, and secure against eavesdroppers.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Intel sells majority stake in Altera to Silver Lake investors in a deal valued at $8.75 billion
item.description: Intel announced Monday that it was selling the majority of its Altera chip business to technology investor Silver Lake in a deal valued at $8.75 billion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Can’t securely login to your PC using facial recognition anymore? New Windows 11 update patch is likely to blame – if you can install the update at all
item.description: Windows 11’s new update is breaking facial recognition logins with Windows Hello, and also simply failing to install.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 7 benchmarked with Android 16 and a top-tier chipset
item.description: The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 7 has appeared on Geekbench, running One UI 8 and with a Snapdragon 8 Elite chipset.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Now companies can patch Windows 11 without rebooting
item.description: Microsoft has now added support for hotpatching in Windows 11 Enterprise 24H2 on x64 systems (AMD/Intel), Bleeping Computer reports. The support means that some security updates can be installed without restarting the computers.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Sony hikes PlayStation 5 console prices for Europe, UK and Australia citing economic turmoil
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Sony said it will raise prices starting Monday for some PlayStation 5 video game consoles in Europe, Australia and New Zealand, citing global economic turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: PS5 Digital Edition Price Increases in Some Regions Amid Economic Challenges
item.description: PlayStation 5 Digital Edition price rises announced for UK, Europe, Australia, and New Zealand due to economic factors
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Samsung’s One UI 7 update reportedly delayed due to a serious bug, extending the wait for Android 15
item.description: One UI 7 users have reportedly had issues unlocking their phones, causing Samsung to halt the update.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Leaked Avengers: Doomsday images seemingly tease meeting between rival X-Men characters – and a Marvel hero who wasn't part of the original cast reveal
item.description: The first set leaks from Avengers 5 have been posted online and they reveal some interesting things about the MCU film.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: How pet projects fuel innovation and careers in tech
item.description: Call them pet projects, side projects, or hobby projects, projects pursued outside of one’s regular business activity are especially popular among software developers. After all, these projects serve as an excellent way to learn new skills and technologies or to test fresh concepts and approaches.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Japanese leader says he hopes Osaka expo will help reunite a divided world
item.description: OSAKA, Japan (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, speaking at Saturday's opening ceremony for the Osaka expo, expressed his hope that the event will help restore global unity in a world plagued by conflicts and trade wars.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA McGILL and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: This great free tool brings Pixel-quality image sharpening to any device
item.description: It really is mind-blowing how much incredible stuff we can do with images these days. ’Twasn’t long ago, after all, that advanced image adjustments required pricey desktop-computer software and super-specific knowledge.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: What it means to be an AI-augmented leader
item.description: Rasmus Hougaard is the founder and managing partner of Potential Project. In 2019 he was nominated by Thinkers50 as one of the eight most important leadership thinkers in the world.
item.dc:creator: Next Big Idea Club, Fast Company

item.title: CIO Sharon Mandell transforms Juniper Networks for the AI era
item.description: CIOs have elevated their stature in the business by driving digital transformations for nearly a decade. Now, top-level IT chiefs are taking this formula a step further by putting digital initiatives to work in helping transform the business itself.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The problem with Google’s Pixel 9a
item.description: Let’s get one thing out of the way first: Google’s Pixel 9a is one heck of a device. I’ve had the latest Pixel gizmo in my man-claws for a couple o’ weeks now, thanks to a loaner review unit provided by Google.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects
item.description: Microsoft said on Wednesday it is "slowing or pausing" some of its data center projects, describing the move as a show of flexibility as the artificial intelligence (AI) industry evolves.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Infoblox, Google Cloud partner to protect hybrid and multicloud enterprise resources
item.description: Infoblox and Google Cloud announced a partnership that powers new products from each company that they say will help enterprise organizations accelerate their cloud adoption with advanced networking and security capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Planning an international trip? Travelers should prepare for the possibility of extra scrutiny of their phones when crossing borders, especially when entering the United States. The Canadian government warned travelers in a recent travel advisory that U.S.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft said it is “slowing or pausing” some of its data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio, the latest sign that the demand for artificial intelligence technology that drove a massive infrastructure expansion might not need quite as many powerful computers as expected.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: IETF hatching a new way to tame aggressive AI website scraping
item.description: For web publishers, stopping AI bots from scraping their best content while consuming valuable bandwidth must feel somewhere between futile and nigh impossible. It’s like throwing a cup of water at a forest fire.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Humanoid robot breakdances its way into history
item.description: Atlas robot by Boston Dynamics amazes tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson with its breakdancing moves.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft workers say they've been fired after 50th anniversary protest over Israel contract
item.description: Microsoft has fired two employees who interrupted the company's 50th anniversary celebration to protest its work supplying artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military, according to a group representing the workers.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Five tips for incoming CIOs under the new administration
item.description: Max Everett, a former Energy Department CIO, offers some advice for how new agency technology leaders can deal with some basic challenges. The post Five tips for incoming CIOs under the new administration first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network
